mobile_ap.summary: Apple extended its chip supply agreement with component suppliers through 2031, securing critical processors for its core products. Qualcomm is expanding its presence across mobile and AI chip markets as investors assess long-term growth potential. New device launches including the Nothing Phone 4b with Snapdragon processors and the Vivo V80 series with Snapdragon 7 Gen 4 demonstrate continued demand for mid-to-premium mobile SoCs.

hpc_datacenter.summary: Major AI chip stocks surged amid record market momentum, with Nvidia inaugurating an expanded R&D center in Beersheva and reportedly recruiting ASIC engineers to compete with Broadcom and Marvell. SK hynix is aggressively hiring across HBM design and AI engineering roles while removing educational barriers for recruitment, reflecting sector-wide talent competition. Anthropic expanded its partnership with Google and Broadcom for multiple gigawatts of next-generation compute capacity, while Intel and Micron continue hiring sprees with new discrete GPU marketing leadership and a planned Atlanta design center respectively.

custom_soc.summary: Broadcom secured a landmark extension of its custom chip supply deal with Apple through 2031, positioning itself as a full-stack provider for AI infrastructure beyond RF components. Multiple AI companies are investing in proprietary silicon: Zhipu AI is exploring custom ASICs as GLM-5.2 usage surges 27x, Meta is shifting $6.5 billion toward Samsung for AI chips, and Anthropic is turning to Samsung for custom AI processors. TAI is building edge AI silicon while Amazon is leveraging legacy GPU inventory amid ongoing silicon constraints.

foundry.summary: TSMC is reportedly demanding 7nm price increases from major customers including Nvidia, AMD, Apple, and Qualcomm ahead of Q2 earnings announcements. Samsung is competing for custom AI chip agreements with hyperscalers including Meta (reportedly $6.5 billion) and Tesla, with CEO Lee Jae-yong attending Sun Valley to strengthen big tech partnerships. Chinese firms are reportedly shifting domestic AI chip budget allocation from 30% to 46% as they move away from NVIDIA reliance.

packaging.summary: Advanced packaging technologies market is projected to surge beyond $15 billion, driven by AI chiplet-based designs requiring sophisticated test and integration solutions. Alpha and Omega Semiconductor introduced AmpStack packaging for enhanced MOSFET power density, while a Chinese semiconductor firm is expanding with a $1.15 billion chip packaging plant. Intel's XBM memory targeting HBM4 specifications at 32 GT/s through UCIe links, and 6G base stations becoming AI computing nodes, are accelerating demand for high-density fan-out panel packaging and advanced encapsulation materials.
